title: Boy playing with gun shoots girl
description: NATIONAL NEWS - A six-year-old girl was...
bodyText:
NATIONAL NEWS - A six-year-old girl was hospitalised after being accidentally shot by an eight-year-old boy in Everest informal settlement on Sunday morning.

The shooting happened when the boy played with a firearm he had found under a pillow while playing with the girl at her home.


> SAPS spokesperson Sergeant Themba Tshabalala said it is alleged that the boy was playing with the firearm when he fired off a shot.


“A shot went off, injuring the victim on her right hand, and the bullet struck her in the shoulder, exiting her back on the right side,” said Tshabalala.

“A 7.65-mm cartridge was found on the scene. The girl was taken to Far East Rand Hospital for treatment.”

Tshabalala said it is alleged that the firearm owner is an electrician who had come to fix electricity at the shack and left it there.

“Another man went to the shack to fetch the firearm after the incident, stating that he was returning it to the owner, who had since run away.”

No arrests have been made.

The police have opened an enquiry for attempted murder that will be registered due to the boy’s age.

Article: Caxton publication, The Citizen

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
